title: Massive Darkness 2 - What am I doing wrong?
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rV23IXWdDOY

Daniel (@dungeondive) posts a short, frustrated plea, invoking the old maxim that the surest way to learn the correct rules is to play a game wrong on YouTube and get corrected. His problem is with Massive Darkness 2's combat, which keeps one-shotting his level-one heroes — something that's happened to him about five times — even though everyone online insists the game is easy.

He sets up a deliberate worst-case to illustrate. His monk, armed with a bow, makes a ranged attack on a Naga mob (a leader plus four minions) several zones away. Reading the rules as written, the mob's attached item card determines its attack type — here a melee sword — yet when he assembles the dice pool, the four black monster dice come up with retaliation slashes that, by the printed rules, deal automatic damage to his hero regardless of range and regardless of any armour worn. The result: a melee-armed mob inflicting four-plus points of unblockable damage across multiple zones, instantly killing his hero on his own attack turn.

It makes no sense to him, so he's genuinely asking the community whether he's misinterpreting something, hoping someone can salvage his opinion of the game.

----

Have you played Massive Darkness 2 — is Daniel misreading the retaliation rules, or does melee-mob retaliation really reach across the whole board?
